workflow.name: Microsoft SQL Server HA existing
workflow.description: Bootstrap and validate Microsoft SQL Server HA from an existing automation runner and target inventory.

data.label: Prepare Microsoft SQL Server inventory
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
apt-get update
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y ansible sshpass
cat >/tmp/microsoft_sql_server-inventory.ini <<'INVENTORY'
[microsoft_sql_server]
10.31.115.11 role=mssql name=microsoft-sql-server-01
10.31.115.12 role=mssql name=microsoft-sql-server-02
10.31.115.13 role=mssql name=microsoft-sql-server-03
INVENTORY

data.label: Install Microsoft SQL Server container runtime
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/microsoft_sql_server-runtime.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
- hosts: microsoft_sql_server
  become: true
  tasks:
    - ansible.builtin.shell: |
        if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          docker --version
          exit 0
        fi
        if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          apt-get update
          DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y docker.io docker-compose-plugin
        elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          dnf install -y docker docker-compose-plugin
        elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          yum install -y docker docker-compose-plugin
        fi
        systemctl enable --now docker
PLAYBOOK
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-inventory.ini /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-runtime.yml

data.label: Render Microsoft SQL Server cluster manifests
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/microsoft_sql_server-deploy.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
- hosts: microsoft_sql_server
  become: true
  tasks:
    - ansible.builtin.file:
        path: /opt/octastack/microsoft_sql_server
        state: directory
        mode: "0750"
    - ansible.builtin.copy:
        dest: /opt/octastack/microsoft_sql_server/compose.yml
        mode: "0640"
        content: |
          services:
            mssql:
              image: mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest
              container_name: octastack-mssql
              restart: unless-stopped
              environment:
                ACCEPT_EULA: "Y"
                MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD: "ChangeMe123!"
              ports:
                - "1433:1433"
              volumes:
                - data:/var/opt/mssql
          volumes:
            data: {}
PLAYBOOK

data.label: Deploy Microsoft SQL Server cluster
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >>/tmp/microsoft_sql_server-deploy.yml <<'PLAYBOOK'
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/microsoft_sql_server/compose.yml pull
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/microsoft_sql_server/compose.yml up -d
    - ansible.builtin.shell: docker compose -f /opt/octastack/microsoft_sql_server/compose.yml ps
PLAYBOOK
ansible-playbook -i /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-inventory.ini /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-deploy.yml

data.label: Document Microsoft SQL Server topology
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
cat >/tmp/microsoft_sql_server-topology.md <<'EOF'
# Microsoft SQL Server HA topology

Members:
- microsoft-sql-server-01: 10.31.115.11 (mssql)
- microsoft-sql-server-02: 10.31.115.12 (mssql)
- microsoft-sql-server-03: 10.31.115.13 (mssql)

This example deploys one Microsoft SQL Server container per member host. Replace the placeholder commands with vendor-supported clustering, TLS, backup, and failover automation before production use.
EOF
cat /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-topology.md

data.label: Validate Microsoft SQL Server cluster
data.command:
set -euo pipefail
ansible -i /tmp/microsoft_sql_server-inventory.ini microsoft_sql_server -b -m shell -a 'docker ps --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"'

data.label: Microsoft SQL Server HA health check
data.command: docker exec octastack-mssql /opt/mssql-tools18/bin/sqlcmd -S localhost -U sa -P ChangeMe123! -C -Q 'SELECT 1'
